_meta.purpose: Canonical speciality vocabulary for the Med Sales Tools, plus the mapping that reconciles the three lists that had drifted apart.
_meta.problem: Three vocabularies existed with nothing reconciling them, matched by exact string equality: MST_DATA.SPECS (30, Stakeholder Mapper), prep-config.specialities (8, 'Help me prepare' dropdown), and free-text supplier.specialities (60 distinct strings across 188 suppliers). Consequence: 3 of the 8 dropdown values matched NO supplier at all (Surgery / theatres, Diabetes, Pathology / cold chain), returning zero competitors silently; and 146 of 188 suppliers (78%) were unreachable from any dropdown speciality.
_meta.canonical: MST_DATA.SPECS (30) is the canonical vocabulary. Chosen by Lou 20/07/2026.
_meta.multiMap: A supplier string may map to MORE THAN ONE canonical speciality where it genuinely spans both (e.g. 'Respiratory / anaesthesia'). Forcing a single pick would make a supplier unreachable from half its real market.
_meta.nhsscKey: TWO structures, and conflating them is the main trap. (1) NHSSC customer-facing CATEGORIES — six, each holding named frameworks — is how you buy. (2) NHS eClass — 23 branches, 284 level-2, 2,522 level-3 — is what the catalogue and every NPC code run on. Both are given per speciality below.
_meta.nhsscStructure: CORRECTION, verified 20/07/2026: NHS Supply Chain 'category towers' NO LONGER EXIST. The 2018 11-tower model is defunct and every tower name with it. NHSSC now runs SIX Category Business Units: Medical Technology; Medical and Surgical Consumables; Diagnostic Equipment and Services; Rehabilitation and Community; Food; Facilities and Office Solutions. Source: https://www.supplychain.nhs.uk/categories/
_meta.buyingCentreWarning: The NHSSC category and the buying centre are NOT the same thing, and conflating them misdirects a rep. Blood collection is the worked example: NHSSC files it under Medical and Surgical Consumables (a consumables category), but the product is specified by the PATHOLOGY / blood sciences department and ordered from Pathology Reception — not by the vascular access clinical team and not by ward materials management. Verified across eight trusts' own published pathology user guides.
_meta.reviewNeeded: Entries with "review": true are genuine judgement calls, not obvious mappings. Confirm before relying on them.
_meta.npcConvention: DO NOT DERIVE CATEGORY FROM THE NPC PREFIX. An earlier working note claimed the 3-letter NPC prefix IS the eClass level-3 code, so the cached catalogue could be categorised from the code alone. That is WRONG as a general rule and was corrected 20/07/2026 against the eClass field on live product pages. COUNTER-EXAMPLES: KFK312 -> KFF (Auto Analyser Accessories); KCM224 -> KBD (Laboratory & Pathology other consumables); FSQ337 -> FVB (Catheter - Central venous). WHAT DOES HOLD: the newer 5-digit series (pattern xxx85nnn) matched its prefix in every case tested — FNG85288->FNG, FVH85219->FVH, FMB85036->FMB, KBD85186->KBD. Older short codes do not. The '100% of 1,476 NPCs match ^[A-Z]{3}[0-9]+$' figure is a FORMAT observation only; conforming to the format does not mean the prefix carries the category. SO: read category from the per-product eClass field, not from the code. A prefix join over the cache would silently mis-file older lines. GBUK's blood collection codes are the newer series (FNG85022-85033) and are safe, but do not generalise from that. OTHER TRAPS: (a) product pages have NO breadcrumb — the 'Home / F - ... / FA - ...' strip is static nav, identical on every page; only the eClass field is real. (b) The sidebar Product Category facet is hard-capped at 200 and on /product-list is GLOBAL, not rescoped by an applied filter; on /search?query=... it IS result-scoped and reliable. (c) Search does not do NPC prefix matching — 'FNG85' returns zero; exact NPC only.
_meta.eClassTopLevel: eClass top level as read from the live catalogue mega-menu: 21 codes — A Provisions, B Staff Clothing, C Patients Clothing & Footwear, D Pharmaceuticals Blood Products & Medical Gases, E Dressings, F Medical & Surgical Equipment, G Patients Appliances, H Chemicals & Reagents, I Dental & Optical Equipment, J Diagnostic Imaging & Radiotherapy, K Laboratory Equipment & Services, M Hotel Services, P Building & Engineering, R Purchased Healthcare, T Furniture Fittings, U Hardware Crockery, V Bedding Linen & Textiles, W Office Equipment Telecomms Computers & Stationery, X Transportation, Y Recreational Equipment & Souvenirs, Z Staff & Patient Consulting Services & Expenses. There is NO L, N, O, Q or S top level in the live catalogue — an earlier note listed 23 branches including L Fuel Light Power Water and S Gardening & Farming, which do not appear in the live menu.
_meta.knownDivergence: FULLY RESOLVED 20/07/2026. Both the architecture problem (MST_DATA in a base64 blob) and the content gap (7 specialities with no products) are fixed. data/products.json now holds all 37 specialities with 217 products, matching this file's 37 exactly. Products for the 7 added specialities (bloodcoll, dermatology, orthotics, waste, mes, estates, food) were authored in the same style as the existing 198, using only existing TPL product types and role keys — no new mst-logic.js code paths needed. dermatology's FW entry is deliberately omitted (its NHSSC/eClass home is still PENDING) and falls back to fwFor()'s generic message rather than a guessed URL. mes and food are the thinnest entries, matching their flagged low relevance to typical Hub members. Verified: 217 P entries validate against real SPECS ids/TPL types/role keys with zero errors; every SPECS id has >=1 product; mst-logic.js executes end-to-end with zero exceptions against the full dataset; live-served copy matches. One real error caught before shipping: the NHSSC slug 'diagnostic-equipment-and-services' 404s — correct is 'diagnostic-equipment'.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Continence / Urology
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Rehabilitation and Community — Urology and Bowel Management; Disposable and Washable Continence Care
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: GP Colostomy/Ileostomy/Urostomy & Continence; FU Urological catheters & valves

canonicalSpecialities.label: Vascular access
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Medical and Surgical Consumables — Intravenous Cannula; Central Venous Catheters; Extension Sets and Lines
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: FV Intravenous cannulas, catheters & accessories; EU Vascular Products

canonicalSpecialities.label: Blood collection / phlebotomy
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Medical and Surgical Consumables — Blood Collection Devices (2024/S 000-033791); Blood Draw Tools and Accessories (2024/S 000-009366; Lot 1 Lancets, Lot 2 Tourniquets, Lot 3 Vein Locators)
canonicalSpecialities.nhsscNote: Lancets sit on a SEPARATE framework — 'Blood Draw Tools and Accessories', 2024/S 000-009366, 37 suppliers. Originally tendered together with the tubes, then awarded as two frameworks. Do not group them.
canonicalSpecialities.sellingNote: Manufacturers sell blood collection alongside vascular access (GBUK Vascular, Greiner Bio-One Preanalytics), which is why it feels like one patch commercially — but the NHS buys them through different people on different clocks. Blood culture BOTTLES are different again: closed-system consumables locked to the installed analyser, bought by direct award. King's/Synnovis switched bioMérieux to BD and stated in the notice that 'there is not a viable or compatible alternative'. The bottle follows the box — a rep cannot win bottle business by competing on the bottle.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: Was entirely absent from both the mapper's 8 vascular products and GBUK's supplier record, despite GBUK being an awarded supplier on the NHSSC Blood Collection Devices framework. Flagged by Lou 20/07/2026.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: FNG Needle blood sampling (tubes, needles, holders, winged sets, lancets); FVH Tourniquets & Armboards; FMB/FMC Syringe arterial blood sampling; HAE Microbiology (blood culture bottles); KBD/KFF laboratory
canonicalSpecialities.placementNote: SITS UNDER VASCULAR ACCESS, with a DIFFERENT stakeholder. Two independent research threads converged on this, and it reverses an earlier working assumption that it belonged under pathology. CATEGORY EVIDENCE (all points one way): NHSSC files Blood Collection Devices under Medical and Surgical Consumables, directly alongside Intravenous Cannula, Central Venous Catheters and Extension Sets — NOT in Diagnostic Equipment and Services where Pathology lives. eClass FNG (Needle blood sampling) sits under F Medical & Surgical Equipment; tourniquets are FVH, literally inside FV vascular access. The NHSSC catalogue tree puts blood collection under Medical > Clinical products, while Medical > Pathology is a separate tree. BD reorganised to match, effective 01/10/2025: its Medical Essentials segment holds exactly two units — Medication Delivery Solutions (vascular access) and Specimen Management (Vacutainer) — having moved Preanalytical OUT of Life Sciences. GBUK does the same: Blood Collection is one of the eight categories inside its Vascular range. THE CAVEAT A REP MUST BE TAUGHT: the category is unified but the BUYING CENTRE is not. Pathology / blood sciences specifies; procurement transacts. Verified in trusts' own published user guides (Hull: requisitions go via the Pathology Supplies Team; Worcestershire: tubes ordered from the laboratory, not general stores). Standardisation has moved up to the pathology NETWORK, so it is increasingly a network-hub decision. Selling tubes to procurement alone loses on specification; selling to pathology alone loses on the route. ONLY blood culture bottles (HAE Microbiology) and lab transport (KBD/KFF) genuinely sit in pathology's branches — and bottles are a closed proprietary consumable travelling with the analyser contract.
canonicalSpecialities.stakeholderOverride: Blood Sciences / Pathology network — NOT the Vascular Access Team, IPC or critical care who own the rest of the vascular families.

canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Diagnostic Equipment and Services — Laboratory Diagnostics, Point of Care Testing and Pathology (2023/S 000-028831). Lot 1 Managed Services and Lot 5 Specimen Collection & Storage are deliberately fenced apart — Greiner and Sarstedt are on Lot 5 and ABSENT from Lot 1, so an analyser managed service does NOT cover collection consumables
canonicalSpecialities.nhsscNote: Lot 1 and Lot 5 are deliberately fenced off from each other — Greiner Bio-One and Sarstedt sit on Lot 5 and are ABSENT from Lot 1. So an analyser managed-service contract does NOT cover specimen collection consumables. An analyser renewal is not a tube opportunity, and vice versa.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: H Chemicals & Reagents (HA, HF, HH); K Laboratory Equipment & Services (KB, KC, KF)

canonicalSpecialities.label: Blood and transfusion
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: NO NHSSC framework found. Blood COMPONENTS come from NHS Blood and Transplant, not NHSSC. Blood COLLECTION is covered — see bloodcoll. (NHSBT split is reasoned, not sourced — UNVERIFIED)
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: DX Blood Products (under D)

canonicalSpecialities.label: Digital and medical IT
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Diagnostics — Medical IT, Digital Diagnostic Solutions. PARTIAL — most trust digital/EPR is bought outside NHSSC
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: WA Computer Hardware & Software; WF Telecommunications

canonicalSpecialities.label: Dermatology and skin health
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: No dedicated NHSSC sub-category found in the six CBUs. Skin antisepsis sits under Medical and Surgical Consumables (Skin Cleansing, Disinfection and Hygiene, 2025/S 000-001018); phototherapy and diagnostic imaging devices route through Diagnostic Equipment and Services. PENDING a dedicated framework check.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: PENDING — not read from the live catalogue. Skin/wound overlap sits in E Dressings; topical agents in D Pharmaceuticals.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: A supplier tagged 'Dermatology AI' had no home and was falling back to imaging/digital. Dermatology is a real clinical speciality with its own buying centre.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Prosthetics and orthotics
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Rehabilitation and Community — Orthotics; frameworks 'Orthotics, Podiatry and Immobilisation'.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: GL Orthotic Products Abdominal External (GLB spinal supports, back braces, spinal jackets); GN Orthotic Products Lower Limb External (GNC lower limb splints). Both read from the live catalogue.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: Was folded into 'Rehabilitation and mobility'. Four suppliers tag themselves 'Prosthetics & orthotics' and it is a distinct NHSSC sub-category with its own clinical service (orthotics departments, podiatry), so it earns its own speciality.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Clinical waste and sharps management
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Medical and Surgical Consumables — 'Clinical and Sharps Waste Management'; related 'Environmental Decontamination'.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: MME Biohazard & Cytotoxic containers (read via the oncology mapping). Wider waste codes PENDING.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: A named NHSSC framework with real rep territory and no speciality covering it. Sharps safety also connects directly to the Sharps Regulations 2013 driver already used across the vascular and blood collection families.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Managed equipment services and finance
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Diagnostic Equipment and Services — 'Finance solutions' and 'Managed equipment services'.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: N/A — a commercial wrapper, not a product class. Equipment inside an MES carries its own clinical eClass codes.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: Not a clinical speciality, but a significant and growing rep route — pathology MSCs, reagent rental and analyser contracts all sit here, and the mapper already teaches that an analyser contract is a different event from a consumables framework. Deliberately labelled so a rep does not mistake it for a clinical category.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Facilities, estates and workwear
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Facilities and Office Solutions (entire CBU) — catering and cleaning equipment; furniture; medical pulp; stationery; uniforms and workwear.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: T Furniture Fittings; U Hardware Crockery; W Office Equipment Telecomms Computers & Stationery; B Staff Clothing; C Patients Clothing & Footwear; V Bedding Linen & Textiles; MK Cleaning Materials (MKJ bleaches, disinfectants). Top-level codes read from the live catalogue menu.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: An entire NHSSC Category Business Unit had no speciality. Note theatre drapes and medical wipes (VE) stay under Theatres, where the clinical buyer sits.

canonicalSpecialities.label: Food and catering services
canonicalSpecialities.nhssc: Food (entire CBU) — ambient food; beverages; covers (retail and patient); fresh and frozen food; ready meals; vending solutions.
canonicalSpecialities.eClass: A Provisions. Top-level code read from the live catalogue menu.
canonicalSpecialities.addedBecause: An entire NHSSC Category Business Unit had no speciality. LOW RELEVANCE to medical device and pharma reps — added for completeness of the NHSSC key rather than because Hub members are likely to sell here. Distinct from 'Nutrition and enteral feeding', which is clinical.

Respiratory / anaesthesia.note: 15 suppliers — the largest split. Genuinely spans both; single-mapping would hide half their market.

ENT / neuro-otology.note: Neuro-otology sits across ENT and neurosciences; mapped to both.

Pathology / transfusion.note: Transfusion labs sit in pathology, but the products are blood/transfusion. Mapped to both.

Endourology.note: Stents, stone management, ureteral catheters — urology. Confirm this is where you'd expect a rep to find it.

Prosthetics & orthotics.note: Split out to its own speciality 20/07/2026, but kept mapped to rehab as well - these were rehab's ONLY four suppliers, and a prosthetics/orthotics supplier genuinely sits in rehabilitation territory. Removing it entirely would have emptied rehab.

Biopsy.note: Biopsy devices are placed under imaging guidance but yield a pathology sample. Ambiguous — your call.

Virtual wards / RPM.note: Remote monitoring is both a monitoring product and a digital platform.

EPR / e-obs.note: Electronic observations feed patient monitoring as well as the EPR.

Dermatology AI.note: Now has a proper home; previously fell back to imaging/digital.

Stroke AI.note: Stroke AI reads CT/CTA imaging for a neuro pathway.

gapsInCanonicalList: Bedding, linen and textiles (eClass V) — folded into 'Facilities, estates and workwear'. Theatre drapes and medical wipes (VE) stay under Theatres, where the clinical buyer is.

Purchased Healthcare (eClass R) — outsourced clinical services. NOT added: it is a service-commissioning route, not a product speciality, and belongs with the ICB commissioning content rather than the product mapper.
Transportation (X), Building & Engineering (P) — NOT added: no realistic overlap with medical device or pharma sales.
Aids for daily living / Technology Enabled Care / wigs / breast prostheses — partially covered by 'Rehabilitation and mobility'; check whether these need splitting out the way orthotics just was.

Closed blood sampling from indwelling lines — genuine white space: no NHSSC framework category and no manufacturer category, exists at product level only (BD VAMP, ICU Medical SafeSet). A product family rather than a speciality, but worth knowing.
